review:
妈妈，人生要是星露谷就好了，没有无奈与绝望，种子种在地上就会成熟，钓鱼永远不会空军，想要什么就自己去争取，每一份努力都会有收获。
妈妈，人生要是星露谷就好了，没有自卑和内耗，你喜欢的人最后也会喜欢你，浪漫和梦想相互交织，你要对抗的只有时间。
妈妈，人生要是星露谷就好了，我可以拥有很多朋友，还有关心我的长辈，永远没有焦虑和煎熬，活在农场的每一秒都是自我价值的实现，就算只是砍树那也是充实的一天。
可是妈妈，我把农场打理的井井有条，和所有人都成为了好朋友，取到了我心爱的妻子，打穿了地下的矿井，学会了祝尼魔的语言，甚至通关了草原之王和祝尼魔赛车，为什么做到这种地步，到头来我也只能一直在一个小房间里打转呢，人生要是星露谷就好了。

I can have many friends and elders who care about me. There will never be anxiety and suffering. Every second of living on the farm is a realization of self-worth. Even if I just cut down trees, it is a fulfilling day. But mom, I took care of the farm in an orderly manner and became good friends with everyone. I got my beloved wife, drilled through the underground mines, learned the Junimo language, and even cleared King of the Grasslands and Junimo Racing. Why did I get to this point? In the end, I can only keep spinning in a small room. If only life were like Stardew Valley.

送出礼物就能得到友谊，失败了可以回档，永远有重来的机会，种下去就一定能长出来的菜，付出努力就一定会有回报，下雨了去海滩钓一整天的鱼也没关系。
就算在床上躺三年什么也不干，评定等级的时候爷爷也只会想着咱能够开开心心享受生活，远离世俗。
人生要真是星露谷就好了。

review:
Купил Stardew Valley по приколу, в итоге в 4 утра обсуждаю с человеком урожай картошки и бегу спасать кур от депрессии.  
Жаль, что далеко не все поймут глубину этой игры)))) действительно тонко)))) не так уж много сейчас людей, которые осознают, что настоящий endgame — это поливать пастернак и молча рыбачить под ночной эмбиент.

Сначала думаешь:
“ну ферма и ферма”

А потом:
— у тебя расписание сна как у тракториста;  
— ты эмоционально зависим от урожая клубники;  
— ты 40 минут выбираешь обои в дом;  
— и искренне радуешься, что виртуальная курица снесла большое яйцо.

10/10  
игра, после которой начинаешь понимать деда.

review:
非常好遊戲，精神時光屋。
但我玩這款遊戲還有另一個原因。以下是我的故事：

我與一位朋友相識於 2020 年。
我們是在某個網頁遊戲裡下棋認識的，後來交換了遊戲外的聯絡方式，斷斷續續聊了很多莫名其妙卻又有趣的話題。
之所以說「斷斷續續」是因為我經常搞消失，訊息愛回不回的。他不一樣，他總是認真回覆我的每一則訊息。
他是個很特別的人。

我們最後一次聊天是在 2024 年。
那時他即將大學畢業，準備迎接人生的下一階段，而我已經工作一段時間了。
他跟我說，他打算刪除現在這個聊天帳號，在那之前，他向我推薦了這款遊戲——《星露谷物語》。
他說，這是一款評價非常好的遊戲，想以此治治我無聊乏味的社畜生活。
但當時的我忙於工作，既沒有心思玩遊戲，也不曾嘗試這種風格，最後便不了了之。
後來，他換了聯絡方式，我們也就此斷了聯繫。

直到最近，2026 年，
我辭職了，突然間閒了下來，百般無聊地逛著 Steam 想找點新遊戲來玩，
無意間，我看見了 Stardew Valley 這個名字。
我想起了他。
我很好奇，當時他口中的星露谷究竟是什麼樣的，於是，我購買了這款遊戲。

我連續玩了一整個下午，甚至一整天。
老實說，這款遊戲比我想像中要好玩得多，這也是我第一次玩這種農場開荒類型的遊戲。
只是玩著玩著，眼淚卻突然掉了下來。
也許是因為遊戲太好玩了，又或者是因為想起了過往的點點滴滴。
說真的，我對他感到抱歉，
抱歉自己沒有珍惜過去相處的時光，也懊悔錯過了最後交換聯絡方式的機會。

他曾說過，他常駐於 Steam 與 Discord，所以我想，在這裡留言也許他能看見。
雖然機會渺茫，又或是要花上很長一段時間，
如果他真的看見了這則評論，那大概是一種奇蹟吧。
如果沒有，那我也祝你幸福。

review:
Se me hace hasta raro reseñar este juego pero lo tengo que hacer. Han sido muy bonitas estas noches de Stardew Valley en las que he encontrado una versión de mí que ni yo conocía. Un juego que deja marca, y bien que la va a dejar... Un juego tranquilo, sin egos, sin preocupaciones; simplemente tú, tu esposa y tus hijos, y tu granja. "Una vida distinta a la nuestra en un juego distinto a los demás".
Sinceramente me ha parecido lo mejor que he podido jugar en estos meses de agobio con el estudio, salvándome más de una noche después de un día estresante. 

El juego en sí es una pasada, empiezas plantando semillitas y acabas con pila millones, pero con la misma humildad y dedicación que la del primer día. El amor que se le pilla a este juego después de cada pez atrapado, cada regalo entregado, cada evento o cada animación de los aldeanos no lo puede entender alguien que no lo haya jugado. Por ello, después de 200 horas y platinármelo, lo recomiendo enormemente, aunque puede llegar a ser obsesivo e incluso ansioso, pero cada momento vivido será un recuerdo que admirarás haber presenciado.

Mis dieces para esta obra de arte y el amor dedicado en ella por parte de Eric.

Pd: Haley no tiene competencia. (no acepto debates)

review_english: It seems strange to me to review this game but I have to do it. These Stardew Valley nights have been very nice in which I have found a version of myself that I didn't even know. A game that leaves a mark, and it will... A calm game, without egos, without worries, just you, your wife and your children, and your farm. "A life different from ours in a game different from the others." Honestly, it seemed like the best thing I've been able to play in these months of study stress, saving me more than one night after a stressful day. The game itself is amazing, you start by planting seeds and end up with millions, but with the same humility and dedication as the first day. The love you get for this game after every fish caught, every gift given, every event or every animation of the villagers cannot be understood by someone who has not played it. Therefore, after 200 hours and platinuming it, I highly recommend it, although it can be obsessive and even anxious, but every moment experienced will be a memory that you will admire having witnessed. My tens to this work of art and the love dedicated to it by Eric. PS Haley has no competition. (I don't accept debates)

review:
プレー開始当初は「あれもできる、これもできる」とか
「あの場所には何があるんだろう」などと先の展開にワクワクするのだが
何をするにも時間と体力を使うので、気が付けば効率を追って自由がなくなっていく
自営業とか自由業と言われる人々の大変さを体験させてくれるゲームだった

登場人物との人間関係構築は結構窮屈
遭遇が面倒なうえに関係を深めようと思えばアイテムの入手やイベント進行などで
プレー管理がさらにタイトになっていく
何より洋ゲーなので一部のキャラは外見や言動でファーストコンタクトから
「コイツと交流するの！？（げんなり）」みたいな気分にさせてくれる

良くできているゲームだとは思うし、色々なコンテンツがあって楽しいと言えば楽しい
おススメか否かと言われればおススメとはいえる
しかし一度何かの機会にこのゲームから離れると、
あの忙しさに戻りたいと思えずそのまま帰ってこれなくなる。私はなった

現実逃避のつもりで始めたゲームで、ゲームの世界から逃げ出してしまった私に
おススメと言う資格はあるのだろうか？

review:
Stardew Valley was one of those games that I had heard about for years and simply had no interest in. It isn't my style, it isn't the type of content I play at all. But one day I was so unbelievably bored that I thought, f*ck it- let's play Stardew Valley. My friends all love it so I might as well.

 I logged 14 hours the first time I opened the game.

The thing is, I could play this game on repeat forever and doesn't even grace my top 3 games. 

It's fun, addictive, wholesome, somewhat dark and even frustrating. The townspeople feel so genuine to be around. I love getting to know them all over again in every run on the game. I'm aiming for 1 save per marriage candidate. 
I love designing my farm, the feeling of euphoria when you reach Ginger Island for the first time... your first batch of starfruit jelly....
I won't consider this a con on the game, but for some reason this is the ONLY game on my pc that crashes. Nothing else, save for maybe a sims crash every fortnight. Anyway. 

Really fun. Going to get a tattoo of a fairy rose when I can.

The only actual cons I can think of is a lack of new diaglouge and events once you finish year 2. Maybe need some new townies or something. Fix Shane's alcoholism after you marry him or something

review:
10年以上前の作品にもかかわらず、いまだに10万人近いアクティブユーザーを抱える化け物タイトル。
今なお新規プレイ動画やMODが作られ続けており、その人気の根強さには驚かされる。

正直に言えば、本作はMOD前提ならおすすめ。
逆に、MODなしのバニラ環境だと、現代基準では快適性に欠ける部分が多く、人によっては序盤で脱落する可能性も高い。

特に気になったのは、
・ 足が遅い
・ イベントスキップや文字送りができない
・ 住人探しだけで1日が終わる
・ アイテム数が膨大なのにインベントリ関連が窮屈
・ 一度入手した素材の入手場所や季節などをゲーム内で確認しづらい
・ イベント発生条件が分かりづらい
・ 農場管理時のUI操作が不便

など。

発売時期を考えれば仕方ない部分ではあるが、現在の快適な作品に慣れていると不便さはかなり感じる。（ストーリーを進めればそれなりに改善される点もある）
実際、私も最初は配信で見る楽しそうな雰囲気と、自分でプレイした時の感触にギャップがあり、早々にプレイを止めようとしていたが、MODを導入してプレイ環境を整えたことで評価が一変した。

移動速度改善、UI拡張、NPC位置表示、インベントリ改善、自動化系など、「こうあってほしい」が大体存在している。しかも、その数がとにかく膨大。

単純なQoL改善から、ゲームバランスを大きく変えるもの、さらにはDLCレベルの追加コンテンツまで存在しており、MOD込みなら拡張性は異常なレベルで高い。

特に本作の強みは、長年愛されてきたことによる圧倒的な情報量とコミュニティの大きさだと思う。

攻略情報、動画、Wiki、MOD、プレイ環境構築など、何を調べても大量に情報が出てくる。
プレイ知識が増えるほど、「情報を調べているだけで楽しい」ゲームでもある。

一方で、MOD導入のハードルは低くない。

Steamワークショップ対応ではなく、基本的に外部サイト経由になるため、慣れていない人にはかなり敷居が高いと思う。
私自身、ゲームバランスを壊さない範囲で「自分に必要なMOD」を精査しながら導入していたら、快適な環境を整えるだけで5時間ほどかかった。

しかもMODは自己責任。

有名サイトや人気MODでも、
・ ゲーム本体アップデートによる不具合
・ MOD同士の競合
・ 環境依存問題

などは普通に起こる。

ただ、MODの大手サイトで、
・ DL数
・ 更新頻度
・ 投稿欄（Posts）
・ SMAPI対応状況

などを確認しながら導入すれば、一般的な利用範囲では安全寄りではあると思う。
それでも、「外部ツール導入」自体に抵抗がある人には向かないかもしれない。

また、本作は見た目のほのぼの感に反して、生々しい人間関係イベントなども割と多い。
純粋な癒し系スローライフを想像していると、少しギャップを感じる人もいると思う。

とはいえ、それらのハードルを越えられれば、今なお多くのプレイヤーを惹きつけ続けている理由はよく分かる。

できること、イベント、やり込み、MOD拡張――その全てが膨大。
ゲーム性が合えば、10年以上遊ばれ続けているのも納得の“人生ゲーム”になり得る作品。
色褪せない普遍的な面白さに寝食を忘れてプレイをしていたのでおすすめです。

review_english: Despite being created over 10 years ago, it's still a monster title with close to 100,000 active users. New gameplay videos and MODs are still being created, and I'm surprised at how popular it is.To be honest, I recommend this game if you're into mods.On the other hand, in a vanilla environment without mods, there are many aspects that lack comfort by modern standards, and some people are more likely to drop out in the early stages.The things that particularly bothered me were: It was slow, and you couldn't skip events or text. You can spend a day just looking for residents. Even though there are a huge number of items, the inventory is cramped. Once you have obtained the materials, it is difficult to check the location and season in the game. It is difficult to understand the conditions for event occurrences. The UI operation when managing the farm is inconvenient. Considering the release date, this is something that can't be helped, but if you are used to the current comfortable game, you will feel that it is quite inconvenient. There are some things that will improve as you progress through the story. In fact, I also initially thought it looked like it would be a fun atmosphere when watching the stream. There was a gap in the feel of the game when I played it, and I was about to stop playing it, but my evaluation completely changed when I installed MODs and set up the play environment.Movement speed improvement, UI extension, NPC location display, inventory improvement, automation system, etc. Most of the things that I would like to see are present, and the number of them is huge.From simple QoL improvements to things that greatly change the game balance, there are even additional content at the DLC level, and if mods are included, the expandability is at an abnormal level.In particular, the strength of this game is I think it's due to the overwhelming amount of information and the size of the community that has been loved for many years. Strategy information, videos, wikis, mods, play environment construction, etc. No matter what you look up, a large amount of information will come up. The more you know about how to play, the more fun it is just looking up the information. On the other hand, the hurdles for introducing mods are not low. Since it is not compatible with the Steam Workshop and basically goes through an external site, I think it is quite difficult for those who are not used to it. Personally, I would have carefully selected the mods that I needed without destroying the balance of the game. It took about 5 hours just to set up a comfortable environment.Moreover, mods are done at your own risk.Even with famous sites and popular MODs, problems caused by game updates, conflicts between mods, environment dependence problems, etc. are common. However, if you install it while checking the number of downloads, update frequency, post section, Posts, SMAPI compatibility, etc. on major MOD sites, I think it is safe for general use. Still, it may not be suitable for people who are reluctant to introduce external tools. Also, contrary to the heartwarming feeling it seems, this game is There are quite a lot of vivid human relationship events, etc. I think some people may feel a bit of a gap when imagining a pure relaxing slow life. However, if you can overcome those hurdles, you can understand why it continues to attract so many players. Things you can do, events, replays, mods, expansions, etc. All of them are huge. If the game quality matches, it can be a game of life that is no wonder why it has been played for over 10 years. I recommend it because I forgot about eating and sleeping while playing because of the timeless and universal fun.

review:
Stardew Valley ilk bakışta “sakin çiftçilik oyunu” gibi duruyor ama aslında içine girince zaman algısını tamamen bozan bir oyun. “Bir gün bakıp çıkayım” diyorsun, bir bakmışsın saatler geçmiş, tarlayı düzenlemişsin, madenin 20 katını inmişsin, kasaba halkıyla ilişki kurmuşsun.

Oyunun en güçlü tarafı kesinlikle özgürlük hissi. Kimse seni zorlamıyor, ne yapacağın tamamen sana kalmış. İstersen çiftçilik yapıyorsun, istersen balık tutuyorsun, istersen madene inip savaşıyorsun. Ama en ilginci, bu basit aktivitelerin bile zamanla aşırı bağımlılık yapması. Bir tane ürün ekiyorsun, sonra “bir sezon daha oynayayım” diyorsun, döngü başlıyor.

Kasaba ve NPC sistemi de oyunu çok canlı yapıyor. Her karakterin kendine ait bir hikayesi, rutini ve kişiliği var. İlk başta sıradan gelen karakterler zamanla oyunun en önemli parçası haline geliyor. Hatta bazı NPC’lerle ilişki geliştirmek oyunun ana hedeflerinden biri gibi hissettiriyor.

Müzikler ve atmosfer inanılmaz rahatlatıcı. Oyun seni stres yapmaya değil, sakinleştirmeye çalışıyor ama bir yandan da “verimli olma” hissi veriyor. Bu ikisinin dengesi çok iyi kurulmuş. Özellikle yağmurlu günlerde kasabada dolaşmak bile ayrı bir keyif.

Ama oyun kusursuz değil. Özellikle erken oyunda para kazanmak biraz yavaş ilerliyor ve bazı mekanikler ilk başta kafa karıştırabiliyor. Ayrıca bazı oyuncular için tempo çok yavaş gelebilir. Aksiyon ya da hızlı ilerleme bekleyen biriysen sıkılma ihtimalin yüksek.

Ama asıl olay şu: Stardew Valley bir oyun değil, bir alışkanlık. Rahatlatıyor, bağ kurduruyor ve fark ettirmeden içine çekiyor. Basit görünen sistemler birleşince inanılmaz bir derinlik oluşuyor.

Genel olarak hem rahatlatıcı hem de bağımlılık yapan çok nadir oyunlardan biri. Ne zaman açsan “bir gün daha oynayayım” dedirten türden.

review_english: Stardew Valley seems like a calm farming game at first glance, but when you actually get into it, it is a game that completely disrupts the perception of time. One day, you decide to go out and see, hours have passed, you have organized the field, descended 20 floors of the mine, and established relations with the townspeople. The strongest aspect of the game is definitely the feeling of freedom. Nobody is forcing you, it's entirely up to you what you do. If you want, you can farm, if you want, you can fish, if you want, you can go down to the mine and fight. But the most interesting thing is that even these simple activities become extremely addictive over time. You plant one crop, then you say let's play for another season, and the cycle begins. The town and NPC system also make the game very lively. Each character has their own story, routine and personality. Characters that seem ordinary at first become the most important part of the game over time. In fact, developing relationships with some NPCs feels like one of the main goals of the game. The music and atmosphere are incredibly relaxing. The game tries to calm you down, not stress you, but at the same time it gives you the feeling of being productive. The balance of these two is very well established. Even walking around the town is a pleasure, especially on rainy days. But the game is not perfect. Earning money is a bit slow, especially in the early game, and some mechanics can be confusing at first. Also, the pace may be too slow for some players. If you are someone who expects action or fast progress, you are likely to get bored. But the main thing is that Stardew Valley is not a game, it is a habit. It relaxes, connects and draws you in without noticing. When seemingly simple systems come together, incredible depth is created. Overall, it is one of the very rare games that is both relaxing and addictive. The kind that makes you want to play one more day every time you open it.

review:
An amazing game that's constantly updated, done almost entirely by a single developer until coding for multiplayer became a bit too tedious. When I say everything, I mean absolutely everything. The music, the graphics, the coding, which is a feat in and of itself. There's plenty of videos detailing the biography behind the development of this game, one more inspiring than the next.

The game itself is simple yet unique and can get intrinsically complex with how to optimize or simplify things. There's plenty of ways to make money, solidify relationships, or simply enjoy music and events in the game.

In essence, it's a farming game that plays on old Harvest Moon vibes, with much more in almost every aspect. This has a niche for every style of gameplay you could ask for. My nieces play it in my car. My Uncles play it in their free time, we always compare our farms whenever we have family gatherings. There's a magic to it that encapsulates every audience, whatever you choose to do in game.

Wenn ich für den Rest meines Lebens nur noch einziges Spiel spielen dürfte, dann wäre es Stardew Valley. Es ist so viel mehr als nur eine Farm-Sim. Es ist ein Liebesbrief an dieses Genre. Nach 10 Jahren gibt es immer noch Updates, die die Welt weiter vergrößern und neue Rohstoffe + Rezepte bringen. Jeder der NPCs hat seine sympathischen Momente und eine Geschichte, durch die man sie besser versteht, wenn man sie erst einmal richtig kennengelernt hat. Ok, außer Morris, der ist ein Arsch. ;-)
Und auch nach über 1200 Spielstunden entdecke ich immer noch irgendwelche kleinen Details, die zeigen wie viel Liebe in die Entwicklung dieses Spiels gesteckt wurde.

review_english: If I could only play one game for the rest of my life, it would be Stardew Valley. It's so much more than just a farming sim. It's a love letter to this genre. Each of the NPCs has their likeable moments and a story that makes you understand them better once you get to know them properly. Okay, except Morris, he's an ass. -) And even after over 1200 hours of playing, I still discover little details that show how much love was put into the development of this game.

review:
Não tenho palavras para descrever como esse jogo mudou a minha vida! Há mais ou menos 5 ou 6 anos, comecei a jogar pelo celular achando que seria só mais um joguinho de fazenda qualquer. Nunca imaginei que me apaixonaria tanto por esse jogo.
Na minha opinião, nenhum jogo consegue recompensar ou punir o jogador por ser (ou não ser) "disciplinado" da forma que Stardew Valley faz. Tipo você regou suas plantas durante 5 dias e agora pode vender e lucrar com elas, ou você esqueceu de dar comida para os seus animais e agora eles estão bravos com você, fazendo com que perca dinheiro naquele dia.
Me impressiona que um jogo tão vivo e atemporal tenha sido criado por apenas um cara! Na Steam, tenho apenas 72 horas de jogo, mas, somando o tempo que passei no celular, já devo ter mais de 300 horas. São 3 fazendas incríveis, 2 esposas e 1 marido, e ainda assim consigo criar uma nova fazenda e ter uma experiência totalmente diferente.

[Uma dica para os iniciantes que estão começando agora: pesquem bastante e comprem batatas até o dia 13. Quando chegar o dia 13, comprem morangos e plantem o máximo que conseguirem!

review_english: I have no words to describe how this game changed my life! About 5 or 6 years ago, I started playing on my cell phone thinking it would be just another farm game. I never imagined I would fall in love with this game so much. In my opinion, no game can reward or punish the player for being (or not being) "disciplined" the way Stardew Valley does. Like you watered your plants for 5 days and now you can sell and profit from them, or you forgot to feed your animals and now they are mad at you, causing you to lose money that day. It amazes me that such a lively and timeless game was created by just one guy! On Steam, I only have 72 hours of play, but, adding the time I spent on my cell phone, I must have had more than 300 hours. There are 3 incredible farms, 2 wives and 1 husband, and I still manage to create a new farm and have a totally different experience. A tip for beginners who are starting now, fish a lot and buy potatoes by the 13th. When the 13th arrives, buy strawberries and plant as many as you can!

[h1] 人生要是星露谷就好了 [/h1]

在这种下的种子总会发芽，努力总有回报。 我可以慢慢修复那个破旧的社区中心，一点一点把小镇变得更好；在星露谷，失败不可怕，错过什么也没关系，因为这里永远有下一个春天。 这里没有内耗，没有得失，只有四季更替、作物成长，和那些会因为你送了简单的礼物就开心的村民。

我已经在这里度过了无数个日子，我的农场规模也越来越大，挖遍了矿洞......可每次回到现实，还是会忍不住想，如果现实也能像星露谷一样，只要我真心付出，就一定会被温柔以待，那该有多好，如果人生不小心出错了，可没有读档重来的机会。

人生要是星露谷就好了。 谢谢你，让我在这个小小的像素世界里，找到了最柔软的梦想。

review_english: h1 It would be great if life were like Stardew Valley h1 Under this kind of environment, seeds will always germinate and hard work will always pay off. I can slowly repair the dilapidated community center and make the town better little by little. In Stardew Valley, failure is not terrible. I still can’t help but think about how great it would be if reality could be like Stardew Valley. As long as I give sincerely, I will be treated with tenderness. If life accidentally goes wrong and there is no chance to start over, it would be great if life could be like Stardew Valley. Thank you for allowing me to find the softest dream in this small pixel world.

review:
Gostei tanto do jogo que acabei platinando, pois achei incrível o game ter objetivos como o centro comunitário e depois a perfeição, isso me deu foco para continuar jogando e o que fazer, diferente de outros jogos de fazendas ou suvivor que o objetivo é só sobreviver. O gráfico é um pixel arte muito bonitinho e a gameplay é simples, porém funciona perfeitamente em tudo que se propõe e isso é em muita coisa, como mineração, plantação, festivais, exploração e por aí vai. A ambientação é muito boa e me fez ficar muito imerso no jogo durante toda minha gameplay, todas as áreas são muito bonitas, apesar de algumas áreas serem bem pequenas e a trilha sonora é muito boa e combina demais com o ambiente, tem músicas que realmente ficam na sua cabeça. E sobre os personagens, todos são bem carismáticos e bem escritos e é muito bom ver a relação que você constrói com eles, realmente todos tem alguma coisinha a oferecer e uma boa história.

Acho que as únicas coisas que poderiam melhorar é o tempo in game que achei curto demais, pois você tá sempre correndo e tira um pouco da vibe cozy, ainda mais no multiplayer. Os festivais são muito simples, gostaria que tivessem alguns com mais premiações e coisas para fazer e mais vezes ao mês e a interação com seus filhos é muito simples também.

review_english: I liked the game so much that I ended up platinum, because I thought it was incredible that the game had objectives like the community center and then perfection, this gave me focus to keep playing and what to do, unlike other farm or survivor games where the objective is just to survive. The graphics are very cute pixel art and the gameplay is simple, but it works perfectly in everything it sets out to do and that's a lot of things, like mining, plantations, festivals, exploration and so on. The ambience is very good and made me very immersed in the game throughout my gameplay, all the areas are very beautiful, although some areas are very small and the soundtrack is very good and matches the environment, there are songs that really stick in your head. And about the characters, they are all very charismatic and well written and it's great to see the relationship you build with them, everyone really has something to offer and a good story. I think the only things that could improve is the time in game, which I found to be too short, as you're always running and it takes away some of the cozy vibe, especially in multiplayer. The festivals are very simple, I wish they had some with more prizes and things to do more times a month and interacting with your children is very simple too.

review:
Lo he jugado un poquito y creo que es un buen juego asi que dejenme que les cuente mi experiencia. 

Ah Stardew Valley, si eres fan de la saga Harvest Moon o cualquier tipo de juego de granjas, no solo has escuchado de este juego sino que probablemente tienes igual o mas horas jugadas que yo. Pero vamos al comienzo, descubri este juego por pura casualidad por un video ya hace varios años y lo que pense que seria un "lo probare un rato para ver que tal" como un juego pirata, se convirtio a "Una hora mas no hara daño, esas papas no se recogeran solas", decir que luego de ponerle unas 30 horas a su version pirata, lo compre y le ♥♥♥♥ 1500 horas mas, creo que habla un poco sobre la calidad y cantidad de contenido que hay en este juego y para este juego (LOS MODS).

Pero bueno, Stardew valley es un juego mas o menos relajado, de una granja a la cual tienes que hacer crecer desde un lugar lleno de hierbas y arbustos, en una productora de melones de alta eficiencia (al menos si me lo preguntas a mi). Tiene un sistema de minas donde encontraras joyas (para regalarselas a los varios NPCs del juego) y minerales con los cuales mejorar tus herramientas. Los NPCs no son cosas del otro mundo, tienen historias tiernas o un poco tragicas, hay de todos gustos, tamaños y colores. El sistema de pesca que hay es infame en la comunidad por ser dificil al parecer, lo cual nunca me a parecido pero quedas avisado.

Y creo que de momento eso es todo lo que puedo decir por Stardew Valley, un excelente juego, no crea la rueda, pero la hace tan pero tan bien, que no puedes no amarlo. Fue un proyecto de pasion por un desarrollador y se nota que hay genuino cariño al genero en este juego. Definitivamente es una compra segura

10/10 En mi proxima vida espero poder jugarlo otra vez por primera vez

review_english: I've played it a little bit and I think it's a good game so let me tell you about my experience. Ah Stardew Valley, if you are a fan of the Harvest Moon saga or any type of farming game, not only have you heard of this game but you probably have the same or more hours played than me. But let's go to the beginning, I discovered this game by pure chance through a video several years ago and what I thought would be a "I'll try it for a while to see how it goes" as a pirate game, became "One more hour won't hurt, those potatoes won't pick themselves up", to say that after putting about 30 hours into its pirate version, I bought it and gave it 1500 more hours, I think it speaks a little about the quality and quantity of content that is in this game and for this game (THE MODS). But hey, Stardew Valley is a more or less relaxed game, about a farm that you have to grow from a place full of herbs and bushes, into a highly efficient melon producer (at least if you ask me). It has a mining system where you will find jewels (to give to the various NPCs in the game) and minerals with which to improve your tools. NPCs are not things from the other world, they have tender or a little tragic stories, there are all tastes, sizes and colors. The fishing system that exists is infamous in the community for apparently being difficult, which has never seemed like it to me but you are warned. And I think that for now that's all I can say for Stardew Valley, an excellent game, it doesn't create the wheel, but it does it so, so well, that you can't not love it. It was a passion project for a developer and you can tell there is genuine love for the genre in this game. It is definitely a safe purchase 10 10 In my next life I hope to be able to play it again for the first time

review:
Stardew Valley is one of those rare games that feels like a second home. Whether you're farming, fishing, mining, decorating, or just talking to the townspeople, there's always something meaningful to do at your own pace. I bought this game thinking I'd play for a few hours. Now I know every villager's schedule, own several sheds full of ancient fruit, and somehow care deeply about virtual chickens.

Years after buying it, I still come back to Pelican Town. Few games offer this much content, charm, and comfort for such a small price. A masterpiece made with love <3

review:
Damm was ein Game. 
Das Spiel kann man entweder wirklich auf tryhard spielen oder wirklich Cozy spielen. Beides ist mgl und es gibt nicht wirklich Stress, das man es nicht schafft den Community Center im einem Jahr oder sonstiges zu schaffen. Man macht sich nur den Stress bzw ist es der Ehrgeiz dahinter. nicht mehr und nicht weniger. 
Ansonsten: 9.5/10 Spiel ist für mich ein Must Play. 
Das einzige was zu kritiisieren könnte wäre das End game, wenn man wöllte. 
Du kannst halt trotzdem viel erledigen, z.B. dein Hof dekorieren, die Stadt dekorieren, mit allen Freundschaften knüpfen, Best way to become rich lol, etc.

Ich persönlich mag den Tryhard way, weil es mir halt den Anreiz bzw Dopamin gibt und deswegen das Early game mehr mag als Late. So Min max stuff bla bla.
Wie gesagt braucht man nicht, kannst den Spaß auch in einem Jahrhundert machen, wenn du Faul bist und einfach entspannen willst.

PS. Die Reise des Prärie Königs...größter Pain bei dem 100% Archivement  Run

review_english: Damn what a game. You can either play the game on tryhard or play it really comfortably. Both are possible and there is no real stress if you don't manage to complete the community center in a year or anything else. You're just stressing yourself out or it's the ambition behind it. neither more nor less. Otherwise 9.5 10 game is a must play for me. The only thing that could be criticized would be the end game, if you wanted. You can still do a lot, e.g. decorate your yard, decorate the city, make friends with everyone, Best way to become rich lol, etc. I personally like the tryhard way because it gives me the incentive or dopamine and that's why I like the early game more than the late game. So Min max stuff blah blah. As I said, you don't need it, you can do it in a century if you're lazy and just want to relax. P.S. The Journey of the Prairie King...biggest pain in the 100 Archive Run

review:
Where do I start, 
Played this game across Multiple Platforms, 
This game has been a Fun and Chill time. A Game I keep coming back to from time to time. With all my hours. I still find new things each time I play, Still Discovering Paths on my own. This game is a Great time for those looking for something to just have a Chill and take things slow with, To Forget the World around them and just focus on the simple things. Life gets hard. Stardew is that game that makes you forget about the world problems you have in the moment just for that few hours or hour in the day to Focus on other things. I have enjoyed every minute of this game and I Cannot wait for the next game Ape has store!

PS The Mulitplayer is also quite Fun, Wasn't sure it would be when it First come out! But it adds a bit to the game :D

playtime_forever: Stardew Valley is my favorite game in the entire world. If I had to pick only one game to play for the rest of my life, it would be this one without hesitation. Whether I'm farming, fishing, mining, decorating my farm, raising animals, making friends with the townspeople, or just enjoying the peaceful atmosphere, there's always something to do. Every playthrough feels a little different, and somehow I still find new things to discover even after countless hours. It's cozy when I need to relax, challenging when I want goals to work toward, and endlessly replayable. The music is beautiful, the characters are memorable, and the amount of love and care that went into this game is obvious in every detail. This isn't just one of my favorite games it's my favorite game of all time. I can't recommend it enough. 10 10, would happily spend the rest of my life on my farm.

review:
Этот шедевр заслужено занимает место среди моих любимых игр. Когда я узнал что над этой игрой кропотливо трудился всего один человек и его отношение к этому процессу мне сразу стало понятно откуда такая качественная проработка сюжета и геймплея в целом! С каждой сменой сезона и прожитых игровых лет в игровой процесс добавляется что-то новое (сюжеты, локации, предметы), всё это не даёт тебе скучать, игра лишена однообразия и ты постоянно думаешь: "что будет дальше?". В игре в целом множество приятных деталей, проработанные диалоги, атмосфера, множественные направления деятельности.

Насколько мне известно, автор так же планирует выпускать ещё обновления, но в основном работает над новой игрой. С нетерпением буду ждать и спасибо за ваш труд!

С каждой сменой сезона и прожитых игровых лет в игровой процесс добавляется что-то новое (сюжеты, локации, предметы), всё это не даёт тебе скучать, игра лишена однообразия и ты постоянно думаешь "что будет дальше?". В игре в целом множество приятных деталей, проработанные диалоги, атмосфера, множественные направления деятельности. Насколько мне известно, автор так же планирует выпускать ещё обновления, но в основном работает над новой игрой. С нетерпением буду ждать и спасибо за ваш труд!
review_english: This masterpiece deservedly takes its place among my favorite games. When I found out that only one person worked painstakingly on this game and his attitude towards this process, it immediately became clear to me where such high-quality development of the plot and gameplay as a whole came from! With each change of season and game years, something new is added to the gameplay (plots, locations, objects), all this does not let you get bored, the game is devoid of monotony and you constantly think “what will happen next?” The game as a whole has a lot of nice details, well-developed dialogues, atmosphere, and multiple areas of activity. As far as I know, the author also plans to release more updates, but is mainly working on a new game. I look forward to it and thank you for your work!

review:
Stardew Valley benim gözümde tam bir başyapıt. Dışarıdan bakıldığında oldukça rahatlatıcı bir oyun gibi duruyor ve dürüst olmak gerekirse ilk 100 saat için gerçekten de öyle. Fakat sonrasında, o kusursuz çiftliğe en hızlı yoldan ulaşmak için elinizde not defteriyle hesap kitap yaparken bulabiliyorsunuz kendinizi; o sakinlik yerini tatlı bir rekabete bırakıyor.

Oyunun temeli basit; ekin ek, hasat et, hayvan bak, balık tut ve madene in. Ancak size sunduğu özgürlük ve çeşitlilik o kadar fazla ki, tamamen kendi isteğinize göre bir yol çizebiliyorsunuz. İsterseniz kazandığınız ilk parayla doğrudan hayvanlara yönelin, isterseniz günlerinizi balık tutarak geçirin ya da tamamen tarıma odaklanın.

Yine de ufak bir uyarım var: Oyunda bir şeyleri aceleye getirip hızlıca yapmaya çalışınca o güzelim tadı biraz kaçıyor. Her şeyi yavaştan almanızı tavsiye ederim, bütün işleri tek bir güne sığdırmak zorunda değilsiniz. Ayrıca oyun görsel olarak ne kadar kolay ve sevimli dursa da, yüzde yüz mükemmelliğe ulaşmak öyle pek de rahatça halledilebilecek bir iş değil.

Son olarak benim tavsiyem; oyunu biraz oynayıp temel mantığını kavradıktan sonra, kesinlikle Stardew Valley Expanded modunu kurup oynayın.

review_english: Stardew Valley is a masterpiece in my eyes. From the outside it seems like a pretty relaxing game, and to be honest, for the first 100 hours it really is. But then, you find yourself doing calculations with a notepad in hand to reach that perfect farm in the fastest way, and that calmness gives way to sweet competition. The basis of the game is simple: plant crops, harvest crops, take care of animals, catch fish and go down to the mine. However, the freedom and diversity it offers you is so much that you can chart a path completely according to your own wishes. If you want, you can focus directly on animals with the first money you earn, spend your days fishing, or focus entirely on agriculture. However, I have a small warning. When you try to rush things in the game and do it quickly, you lose some of its beautiful taste. I recommend taking things slowly, you don't have to fit all the work into a single day. Moreover, no matter how easy and cute the game looks visually, achieving one hundred percent perfection is not a task that can be easily accomplished. Finally, my advice is that after you play the game a little and understand its basic logic, definitely install and play the Stardew Valley Expanded mod.

review:
Before I played Stardew Valley, I never thought pixel art could captivate me. I always gravitated toward modern, realistic graphics, the kind that try to impress you with detail and fidelity. But Stardew Valley changed that completely. Its simplicity isn’t a limitation, it’s a kind of magic. The pixel art feels warm, nostalgic, and alive in a way I didn’t expect, and it opened my eyes to an entire style of games I used to overlook.

I love this game so much that I actually own three copies, one on Switch, one on PC, and one on mobile. Even though I haven’t played much on PC or mobile yet, my Switch farm has over 500 hours poured into it. It’s perfected, polished, and complete… and yet, I still feel the pull to go back. There’s something comforting about stepping onto that farm again, hearing the soft music, and slipping back into the rhythm of planting, harvesting, mining, and connecting with the townsfolk.

What makes Stardew Valley special is how personal it becomes. It doesn’t rush you. It doesn’t demand anything. It simply invites you to build a life at your own pace and somehow, that quiet freedom becomes deeply meaningful. Whether it’s the changing seasons, the relationships you build, or the satisfaction of watching your farm evolve, the game creates a sense of home that’s rare to find.

Even after “finishing” it, I still want to revisit it often. That’s the mark of a truly great game: one that stays with you long after the goals are completed.

Whether it s the changing seasons, the relationships you build, or the satisfaction of watching your farm evolve, the game creates a sense of home that s rare to find. Even after finishing it, I still want to revisit it often. That s the mark of a truly great game one that stays with you long after the goals are completed.

review:
I had mixed feelings in my first couple hours... It’s a “figure things out yourself and learn from your mistakes” type of game since there are little to no tutorials (except if you look at the wiki). Farming and mining is fun but time goes by way too fast each day imo, and your energy gets depleted very easily in the early game. I know it's meant to be played at your own pace, but I personally didn’t enjoy spending so much time walking around, which felt like a chore because of how far you have to go just to do something that takes up half the day. I like maximizing each day and being as productive as possible instead.

3 years later, I decided to give it another chance, this time with mods, and oh boy, I am hooked. Like, wdym I could just fast travel around town to avoid the walking? Plus the energy regeneration mod is a game changer. I don’t have to worry anymore about barely getting anything done in a day. Felt sorry I underestimated the game TT

review:
Um dos melhores jogos que eu já joguei, divertido, viciante, emocional. Não vou ser a primeira nem última review que vai elogiar esse jogo.
 Interessante que a parte "principal", ou seja, a plantação de verduras e frutas, é uma das partes que pode acabar sendo a menos interessante, eu gosto, mas coisas como as minas (meu xodó) e a pesca (que não é tão ruim quanto muitos dizem) acabam pegando minha atenção mais.
 A relação com os integrantes da vila Pelicanos é uma das partes principais do jogo, a maioria dos personagens são bem escritos e tem suas próprias personalidades e peculiaridades. Adoro o Harvey (meu marido no meu save principal), mas admito que ele não é tão interessante quanto outras opções de romance (não recomendo o romance com o Shane, melhor só como amigo).
 Em conclusão; ótimo jogo com gameplay divertida e histórias boas.

Adoro o Harvey (meu marido no meu save principal), mas admito que ele não é tão interessante quanto outras opções de romance (não recomendo o romance com o Shane, melhor só como amigo). Em conclusão ótimo jogo com gameplay divertida e histórias boas.
review_english: One of the best games I've ever played, fun, addictive, emotional. I won't be the first or last review that praises this game. Interestingly, the "main" part, that is, the planting of vegetables and fruits, is one of the parts that may end up being the least interesting, I like it, but things like the mines (my favorite) and fishing (which isn't as bad as many say) end up catching my attention more. The relationship with the members of the Pelican village is one of the main parts of the game, most of the characters are well written and have their own personalities and peculiarities. I love Harvey (my husband in my main save), but I admit that he is not as interesting as other romance options (I don't recommend romance with Shane, better just as a friend). In conclusion, great game with fun gameplay and good stories.

review: I had this game originally on the switch and now I bought it for steam. This game is one that I always come back to, no matter how long it has been since I last played it. It perfectly encapsulates the vibes of the seasons through the environment, music, and festivals for each one.  There is so much to this game that allows for many replays. You can try out different farm types and designs, experience relationships with various characters and, if you're strong enough, abandon the small town vibes for the corporate Joja. If you're into perfecting games, this one has so much to collect and complete to eventually reach that perfection. One of the things that gives this game so much longevity is the ability to play it in small or long chunks. Sometimes I want to sit and play for a few hours, and sometimes I just want to play one in-game day when I have the time. It makes it easy to keep coming back to the game each day. I absolutely adore this game and would recommend it to anyone.

review:
Stardew Valley will always have a special place in my heart.

I first played it in 2018, knowing nothing about it except that it looked cute. I didn’t expect it to become one of my favorite games ever.

I didn’t even need to finish the game to love it. I kept making new farms because every playthrough feels fun, relaxing, and different. When I got my Switch Lite in 2021 (pre-Steam Deck era), this was the first game I bought again just so I could play anywhere.

There’s so much to do like farming, fishing, mining, decorating, building relationships, discovering secrets, and the NPCs are genuinely lovable. It’s a cozy game… until Skull Cavern humbles you.

What makes it even better is the creator, Eric Barone, still releases huge FREE updates for a game that’s already insanely worth the price.

This is the definition of “just one more day” until you realize it’s suddenly 2AM in real life. 

P.S. Oh, and the soundtrack in the game absolutely slaps.

review:
10/10: Escapé del capitalismo para convertirme en esclavo de una calabaza
Compré este juego pensando: "Oh, qué lindo, un jueguecito de granjas relajante para desconectar de la vida real". Falso. Esto es un simulador de explotación agraria superintensiva disfrazado de píxeles adorables y música chill.

Mi resumen tras arruinar mi ciclo de sueño:

El sueño laboral: El juego empieza contigo escapando de un trabajo de oficina explotador (Joja)... para terminar trabajando de 6:00 AM a 1:50 AM todos los malditos días en tu propia granja. Mi personaje no duerme, simplemente parpadea muy fuerte durante 4 horas y vuelve a regar chirivías.

Relaciones interpersonales súper realistas: Descubrí que la mejor manera de hacer que alguien se enamore de ti es acosarlo en su ruta diaria y entregarle exactamente dos botes de mayonesa a la semana. Ah, y me casé con la gótica del pueblo a base de regalarle cuarzo. ¡Y se lo come! Literalmente dice "Mmm, qué rico, tenía hambre".

El sistema de salud: Si te desmayas por agotamiento en tu propio porche a las 2:00 AM, alguien te roba dinero y te lleva a casa. Y si te desmayas en las minas porque una serpiente voladora verde te reventó a golpes, el encantador médico del pueblo te cobra la mitad de tus ahorros vitales por ponerte una tirita.

Odio irracional: Nunca pensé que llegaría a odiar tanto a un NPC, pero Pierre, el dueño de la tienda, es mi némesis. Revende mis cultivos de alta calidad como si los hubiera cultivado él, pero si le vendo algo podrido, le dice a todo el pueblo que es culpa mía.

"Me compré esto para relajarme y terminé haciendo hojas de cálculo en Excel para optimizar el ratio de beneficio de la cosecha de arándanos de otoño."

En conclusión: Llevo 300 horas sin ver la luz del sol en el mundo real, he descuidado a mis amigos de verdad, pero mi granja de cerdos produce aceite de trufa de calidad iridio y los duendecitos del bosque me respetan.

Recomiendo encarecidamente este secuestro voluntario. Compradlo, pero despedíos de vuestra vida social.

review_english: 10 10 I escaped capitalism to become a slave to a pumpkin I bought this game thinking "Oh, how nice, a relaxing little farming game to disconnect from real life." Fake. This is a super-intensive farming simulator disguised as adorable pixels and chill music. My Recap After Ruining My Sleep Cycle The Work Dream The game starts with you escaping an exploitative office job (Joja)... to end up working from 6:00 AM to 1:50 AM every damn day on your own farm. My character doesn't sleep, he just blinks really hard for 4 hours and goes back to watering parsnips. Super Realistic Interpersonal Relationships I discovered that the best way to make someone fall in love with you is to harass them on their daily route and deliver exactly two jars of mayonnaise a week. Oh, and I married the town goth by giving her quartz. And he eats it! It literally says "Mmm, that's delicious, I was hungry." The Health System If you pass out from exhaustion on your own porch at 2:00 AM, someone steals your money and takes you home. And if you faint in the mines because a green flying snake beat you to death, the charming town doctor charges you half of your life savings to put a Band-Aid on. Irrational hatred I never thought I would hate an NPC so much, but Pierre, the shop owner, is my nemesis. He resells my high-quality crops as if he grew them, but if I sell him something rotten, he tells the whole town that it's my fault. "I bought this to relax and ended up making Excel spreadsheets to optimize the profit ratio for the fall blueberry harvest." In Conclusion I haven't seen sunlight in the real world for 300 hours, I've neglected my real friends, but my pig farm produces quality iridium truffle oil and the woodland elves respect me. I highly recommend this voluntary kidnapping. Buy it, but say goodbye to your social life.

review:
I originally bought Stardew Valley a few years back on my switch and it quickly became one of my favorite games to spend hours of my time on. I share the switch with my brother so now that he's currently keeping it, I recently went on to buy it again on here just so that I could play more, and I already have 100 hours just on Steam. I even bought it for my friend because I think it's really a game worth spending money on, and I was happy both to share it with someone else and to support ConcernedApe the goat because he's not a big fat greedy major studio o7 ! (where is this music coming from?)
I've always kind of rushed my gameplays in Stardew, and it took me a while to understand that there wasn't any huge benefit to playing that way, when the game literally punishes you for overexerting yourself etc.. I don't know how many people need to hear this but I think that Stardew doesn't need to be played that way, and I would honestly recommend all other casual players to take it easy. In the end, there's no need to rush for the community center in year 1 and then perfection as soon as possible, and I realised that once I forced myself to slow down a little when playing. Of course, having one or two min max saves is fun, but I'd say to give yourself an opportunity to play it like the """"cozy"""" game ppl claim it is (I hate that word but.)
Haunted Chocolatier tomorrow ok bye ,

review: Всі кричать який це шедевр, яка ж це чудова, цікава і душевна гра!!! Хоча на ділі - це ну дууууже нудна гріндилка. Нічого проти немаю, але в інших пісочницях грінд хочаб цікавий, як в умовній Терарії та навіть Майнкрафті. Гра мееееегааа повільна, прям дуууууже повільна і тобі треба буде бігати туда сюда 248375857 разів, а ще прив'язка нпс до часу і тобі приходиться 2 хв чекати, щоб П'єр двері відкрив... оХ бл1!!. Тут же щей над тобою таймер, як в масці маджорі, і тобі треба все б!!!!ь встигнути, бо не дай бооже ти не встигнеш лягти спати... (VERY RELAXING GAME 10/10). Декор - скудний, персонажі - мідятіна, після клубу гра перетворюється в ще більшу душніну. Не розумію, звідки в цієї гри стільки фанатів. 5 із 10 і то з натяжкою, за гарненький візуал і музику.

review_english: Everyone shouts what a masterpiece this is, what a wonderful, interesting and soulful game it is!!! Although in reality it is a very boring grindstone. I have nothing against it, but in other sandboxes the grind is at least interesting, as in conventional Terraria and even Minecraft. The game is soooo slow, really really slow and you will have to run here and there 248375857 times, and also the nps is tied to time and you have to wait 2 minutes for Pierre to open the door... oH bl1!!. Right now, a timer is watching over you, like in Majora's mask, and you have to be on time, because God forbid you won't have time to go to sleep... (VERY RELAXING GAME 10 10). The decor is poor, the characters are dull, after the club the game becomes even more stuffy. I don't understand why this game has so many fans. 5 out of 10 and that's a stretch, for nice visuals and music.

review:
今天看到星露谷之前购买收据 游戏是2024.7月10日买的 7月11让给前任发了一份 当时和前女友一起玩的 2025年7月30就彻底分手了 8月4号彻底删除 之后也没怎么玩过 今天2026.5月15和室友玩了一天 再次看着熟悉的画面 好像又回到了以前 那会我和前任都不会玩 到处搜攻略 不想浪费每一天 每天早上起来就是浇水 有钱就去买大把大把的种子 靠种植发家致富 种的太多 那时候我们没有酒水器 每天起来就刷电视 看见第二天是下雨不用浇水我俩就特别高兴 对着屏幕傻乐 我特别喜欢把钱攒下来 去建个好看的小房子修个漂亮的大农场 我常说我们有钱多买点种子多浇点水 就能多卖点钱 就能建个和人家一样漂亮的农场 盖个鸡舍 抱几只鸡回来 之前鸡的名字取的还是她名 我特别喜欢去她的联机小屋偷家具 她发现后又去我家把家具抱回来 后面她干脆在门口写个我的名字与狗不得入内！！！我又学她在我的门口写上她的名字不得入内 现在想想 真有意思 我们一起玩星露谷的时候 她还在我身边 天天都能瞧见 那时候很开心 我们还一起玩了隔壁的双人成行 通了关 可惜最后她的选择毁了拥有的一切 我只能说恋爱期间因为新人动摇 拿新人对比现任 借新人推动分手 就是情感背叛 那么对方的说辞只是美化自己 过去挺久的一些事情了 在这个评测写这些也不是放不下那些 只是现在的自己很怀念那时候的日子 想在一张纸上印上自己此刻的想法
在结束了那段感情后 我又恢复了自己原来的样子 大方的承认结束这一段失败的关系
最后 星露谷物语是我认为很优秀很耐玩 好玩的游戏 游戏本身我不多说了 它的评分足够说明它的优秀 最后我还想说
世间没有什么那么多遗憾 好的生活还在前面等着自己去寻 人生南北多歧路 君向潇湘我向秦
珍惜当下， 枯树生花。
review_clean: 今天看到星露谷之前购买收据 游戏是2024.7月10日买的 7月11让给前任发了一份 当时和前女友一起玩的 2025年7月30就彻底分手了 8月4号彻底删除 之后也没怎么玩过 今天2026.5月15和室友玩了一天 再次看着熟悉的画面 好像又回到了以前 那会我和前任都不会玩 到处搜攻略 不想浪费每一天 每天早上起来就是浇水 有钱就去买大把大把的种子 靠种植发家致富 种的太多 那时候我们没有酒水器 每天起来就刷电视 看见第二天是下雨不用浇水我俩就特别高兴 对着屏幕傻乐 我特别喜欢把钱攒下来 去建个好看的小房子修个漂亮的大农场 我常说我们有钱多买点种子多浇点水 就能多卖点钱 就能建个和人家一样漂亮的农场 盖个鸡舍 抱几只鸡回来 之前鸡的名字取的还是她名 我特别喜欢去她的联机小屋偷家具 她发现后又去我家把家具抱回来 后面她干脆在门口写个我的名字与狗不得入内 我又学她在我的门口写上她的名字不得入内 现在想想 真有意思 我们一起玩星露谷的时候 她还在我身边 天天都能瞧见 那时候很开心 我们还一起玩了隔壁的双人成行 通了关 可惜最后她的选择毁了拥有的一切 我只能说恋爱期间因为新人动摇 拿新人对比现任 借新人推动分手 就是情感背叛 那么对方的说辞只是美化自己 过去挺久的一些事情了 在这个评测写这些也不是放不下那些 只是现在的自己很怀念那时候的日子 想在一张纸上印上自己此刻的想法 在结束了那段感情后 我又恢复了自己原来的样子 大方的承认结束这一段失败的关系 最后 星露谷物语是我认为很优秀很耐玩 好玩的游戏 游戏本身我不多说了 它的评分足够说明它的优秀 最后我还想说 世间没有什么那么多遗憾 好的生活还在前面等着自己去寻 人生南北多歧路 君向潇湘我向秦 珍惜当下 枯树生花
review_english: Today I saw the purchase receipt for Stardew Valley. The game was purchased on July 10, 2024. I gave a copy to my ex on July 11. I played it with my ex-girlfriend at the time. We broke up completely on July 30, 2025. It was completely deleted on August 4. I haven’t played much since then. I played with my roommates for a day today on May 15, 2026. I looked at the familiar screen again and it seemed like I was back in the past. At that time, my ex and I didn’t know how to play. I searched everywhere for guides. I don’t want to waste every day. Every morning when we got up, we would water the water. If we had money, we would buy lots of seeds and make a fortune through planting. We planted too much. At that time, we didn’t have a drinking fountain. We would watch TV every day when we got up. We were very happy when we saw that it rained the next day and we didn’t need to water. We were silly and happy in front of the screen. I especially like to save money to build a nice little house and build a beautiful big farm. I often say that if we have more money, buy more seeds and water more, we can sell more money, and we can build a farm as beautiful as others and build a chicken coop. I brought back some chickens. The chickens were named after her before. I especially like to go to her online cabin to steal furniture. After she found out, she went to my house to bring the furniture back. Later, she simply wrote my name on the door and said that dogs are not allowed. I followed her and wrote her name on my door. No dogs allowed. Now think about it, it is really interesting when we played Stardew Valley together. She was still with me every day. I was very happy at that time. It's a pity that her choice in the end ruined everything she had. I can only say that she was shaken by the new person during the relationship. Comparing the new person with the current one and using the new person to promote the breakup is emotional betrayal. Then the other party's words are just beautifying some things that happened in the past. It's not that I can't let go of those things in this review. It's just that I miss those days very much now and want to print my thoughts at the moment on a piece of paper. After the relationship ended, I returned to my original self and generously admitted that I ended this failed relationship. In the end, Stardew Valley is a game that I think is excellent, playable and fun. I won’t say much about the game itself. Its rating is enough to show its excellence. Finally, I would like to say that there is nothing in the world with so many regrets. A good life is still waiting for you to find it. Life has many different roads from north to south. You go to Xiaoxiang and I go to Qin. Cherish the present and flowers will grow on dead trees.

review: The NPCS need more dynamic dialogues that reflect your accomplishments. For example, if you start dating Haley, Emily or any other npc dont even acknowledge it. Also the reactions to gifts are the same no matter your relationship and amount of hearts. Furthermore, every npc only has like 8 different lines when you talk to then (Not considering the ones after you enter a relationship) and it gets old after a few seasons. It's still a great game but there's no reason not to add more dialogues.

review: Stardew Valley est le parfait jeu cozy et il mérite tout son succès. Le jeu est rythmé de façon à ce que le jeu ait un gameplay lent, mais fun tout en ayant de nouvelles features qui apparaissent sans qu'il n'y ait de coup de mou dans la progression et tout ça durant près de 160h pour ma part. Les personnages sont vraiment attachants, complexes et bien écrits surtout lors des events des paliers de cœurs, même si parfois, on sent que les dialogues se répètent, tout particulièrement avec le PNJ auquel vous vous êtes mariés. Et que dire de la direction artistique et de la soundtrack qui sont tout simplement sublimes, vraiment aucun défaut de ce côté. Ce jeu vous tiendra un très long moment, il vaut largement le coup pour son prix, surtout de nos jours. Je recommande chaleureusement.

review_english: Stardew Valley is the perfect cozy game and it deserves all its success. The game is paced so that the game has slow but fun gameplay while having new features that appear without there being any slack in progression and all this lasting almost 160 hours for me. The characters are really endearing, complex and well written, especially during the heart level events, even if sometimes, we feel that the dialogues are repeated, especially with the NPC you married. And what about the artistic direction and the soundtrack which are simply sublime, really no faults on this side. This game will last you a very long time, it's well worth it for the price, especially these days. I warmly recommend.

review: Fun game, really cozy and cute; great to unwind after dopamine dispenser typo games market serves nowadays. It meant to be slow and chill experience but my inner min-maxer couldn't get enough of alt-tabbing between game and wiki trying to optimize every season and mechanic, meaning that everyone could find something in it to try it and sink few hours, or even few more. Also, the soundtrack is mint, visuals are on point, and the gameplay loop is addictive with that 'only one more day' syndrome, and before you notice you spent 2 hours more than you meant. Super fun to do perfectionist 100% run (except praire king ofc). Go ahead and buy it, you won't regret it! :)

review:
Stardew Valley это ад для СДВГшника, а если у вас нет СДВГ то у вас оно появится. Игра вываливает на тебя такое количество активностей, что просто начинаешь теряться и постоянно отвлекаться, а страница ВИКИ станет частью вашего геймплея ибо понять все механики своими силами займет столько времени что страшно представить. Ты хочешь заниматься всем в этой игре, но тебе просто не хватает времени и полоски выносливости. Поля, животные, шахта, готовка, уборка и постоянная поддержка своей фермы в функционирующем состоянии, ведь у тебя постоянно ломаются заборы, дорожки, внезапно появляются камни и ветки, урожай с каким-то шансом просто вянет без причины. Просто потому-что! Заполнение узелков в клубе, квесты от персонажей, 2 дашборда, даже 3... Сменяется время года, меняются маршуты НПС, уходят и приходят новые дикоросы, семена, деревья и рыба. Торговцы могут просто не выйти на работу или уйти на другую активность. А взаимоотношения между персонажами высосут из тебя все силы и эмоции которые оставались после действий выше. Это как донт старв, только ты не умираешь если в чем то ошибся, ты просто теряешь время. А, ну еще и магия в игре есть, которая абсолютно не вписывается в мир и бесит меня. Я ненавижу эту игру. Я наиграл 43 часа и все еще не могу расслабится в этой игре ибо дел и целей не становится меньше. Я еще забыл про рыбалку, да, рыбалка еще есть... А еще надо не забывать гладить животных что бы они на тебя не обиделись. БОЖЕ ЭТО НЕ ИГРА ЭТО РАБОТА! Очень быстро забываешь про внешнюю красоту и успокаивающую атсмосферу игры, ты постоянно бегаешь от точки к точке ибо время жмет. Я однозначно забыл упомянуть про какой-то аспект игры, потому что голова уже кипит, но поверьте, то что я рассказал это не все что есть в игре. 

Я думал что это игра в которой я смогу отдохнуть и просто остановится от внешней суеты, но, внешняя суета проще чем список дел в этой игре.

review_english: Stardew Valley is hell for an ADHD person, and if you don't have ADHD, you will get it. The game dumps so many activities on you that you just start to get lost and constantly distracted, and the WIKI page will become part of your gameplay because understanding all the mechanics on your own will take so much time that it’s scary to imagine. You want to do everything in this game, but you just don't have enough time and stamina bar. Fields, animals, a mine, cooking, cleaning and constant maintenance of your farm in a functioning state, because your fences and paths constantly break down, stones and branches suddenly appear, the harvest, with some chance, simply withers for no reason. Just because! Filling out nodes in the club, quests from characters, 2 dashboards, even 3... The seasons change, NPC routes change, new wild plants, seeds, trees and fish leave and come. Traders may simply not go to work or go to other activities. And the relationships between the characters will suck out of you all the strength and emotions that remained after the actions above. It's like dont starv, only you don't die if you make a mistake, you're just wasting time. Oh, and there’s also magic in the game, which absolutely doesn’t fit into the world and infuriates me. I hate this game. I've played 43 hours and still can't relax in this game because there are no fewer things to do and goals to do. I also forgot about fishing, yes, there is still fishing... And you also have to remember to pet the animals so that they don’t get offended by you. GOD IT'S NOT A GAME IT'S WORK! Very quickly you forget about the external beauty and calming atmosphere of the game, you are constantly running from point to point because time is running out. I definitely forgot to mention some aspect of the game, because my head is already racing, but believe me, what I said is not everything that is in the game. I thought that this was a game in which I could relax and just stop from the external hustle and bustle, but the external hustle and bustle is easier than the to-do list in this game.

review: Ya know what?.... As I look upon this game and it's 98% Overall rating, I don't need to sit here and explainnnnnnnnnnnn to you how much of a masterpiece this game is. It doesn't matter what you are doing, all you know is you are excited to see what happened the next day, the adventure and gathering of your crops and animal products, the running to and from places trying to get there in time and even the little sound effects where every action you do makes your crotch tingle just a little. review:
爷爷留下的那片农场，好似一场永恒的大梦。当你从Joja的格子间里拆开那封信，然后你头也不回的走了。星露谷最美好的地方，不是它给了你一片地，而是它让你心甘情愿地留在那片地里，播撒、收获。春天的防风草，夏天的蓝莓，秋天蔓越莓沼泽的落日，冬天矿洞里的飞蛇.......你都说不好自己到底是在种这片土地，还是在被这片土地种。然后你发现，你其实从来没把这里当游戏，你当它是日子过。
而日子是不需要结局的

review_english: The farm that grandpa left behind is like an eternal dream. When you open the letter from Joja's cubicle, you leave without looking back. The most beautiful thing about Stardew Valley is not that it gives you a piece of land, but that it allows you to stay in that piece of land willingly, sow and harvest. Parsnips in spring, blueberries in summer, sunset in cranberry bog in autumn, flying snakes in mines in winter... You can't even tell whether you are farming this land. You are still being cultivated by this land. Then you find that you have never regarded this place as a game. You have regarded it as a life, and life does not need an ending.

review: While everyone calls it a cozy game, it's pretty overwhelming in regards to the amount of stuff there is to do. But once you get past that, it's probably one of my favorite games to come back to, especially with friends. The combat is pretty fun, though the energy gauge stops you from really getting into a groove with it, but you can't be too upset when you've got some of the most charming NPCs that games have to offer. Ironically enough, it's the farming that's the least interesting part of this farming simulator. Keep a Wiki at the ready for which characters like which gifts, and then give it a go! I've got about 25 hours (so far) on my PS5 copy in addition to the stats I have on Steam.

review:
Stardew Valley é o típico "jogo-conforto". Daqueles que você abre para relaxar, cuidar da fazenda, pescar, minerar, plantar, conversar com os moradores e simplesmente existir em um mundo mais simples e acolhedor. Não precisa de pressa, não precisa de estresse, não precisa provar nada para ninguém.

O mais impressionante é como ele parece pequeno no começo, mas vai revelando uma profundidade enorme aos poucos. Tem gerenciamento, exploração, relacionamentos, eventos, segredos, progressão, personalização e uma liberdade muito boa para jogar no seu próprio ritmo.

A trilha sonora é ótima, o visual pixelado envelheceu muito bem e o ciclo das estações dá uma sensação constante de passagem do tempo. Cada dia dentro do jogo parece simples, mas quase sempre você termina pensando: “só mais um dia”. E quando percebe, jogou horas.

Stardew Valley não é apenas um simulador de fazenda. É um jogo sobre rotina, comunidade, cuidado e recomeço. Um jogo simples na aparência, mas enorme no impacto.

Recomendadíssimo. Já pode ser chamado de clássico moderno.
review_clean: Stardew Valley é o típico "jogo-conforto". Daqueles que você abre para relaxar, cuidar da fazenda, pescar, minerar, plantar, conversar com os moradores e simplesmente existir em um mundo mais simples e acolhedor. Não precisa de pressa, não precisa de estresse, não precisa provar nada para ninguém. O mais impressionante é como ele parece pequeno no começo, mas vai revelando uma profundidade enorme aos poucos. Tem gerenciamento, exploração, relacionamentos, eventos, segredos, progressão, personalização e uma liberdade muito boa para jogar no seu próprio ritmo. A trilha sonora é ótima, o visual pixelado envelheceu muito bem e o ciclo das estações dá uma sensação constante de passagem do tempo. Cada dia dentro do jogo parece simples, mas quase sempre você termina pensando só mais um dia . E quando percebe, jogou horas. Stardew Valley não é apenas um simulador de fazenda. É um jogo sobre rotina, comunidade, cuidado e recomeço. Um jogo simples na aparência, mas enorme no impacto. Recomendadíssimo. Já pode ser chamado de clássico moderno.
review_english: Stardew Valley is the typical "comfort game". The kind you open to relax, tend to the farm, fish, mine, plant, talk to the locals and simply exist in a simpler, more welcoming world. You don't need to rush, you don't need stress, you don't need to prove anything to anyone. The most impressive thing is how it seems small at first, but gradually reveals enormous depth. It has management, exploration, relationships, events, secrets, progression, customization and great freedom to play at your own pace. The soundtrack is great, the pixelated visuals have aged very well and the cycle of seasons gives a constant feeling of time passing. Each day in the game seems simple, but you almost always end up thinking about just one more day. And when you realize it, you've played for hours. Stardew Valley isn't just a farming simulator. It's a game about routine, community, care and starting over. A simple game in appearance, but huge in impact. Highly recommended. It can now be called a modern classic.

Heck, you could even just chill in the bar and play journey of the prairie king. The Co-op in this game is incredible. Two of my friends and I recently started and there's so much room for competition or personal goals. Five star game, no doubt.

review:
10年経ってもたまに初めからやると楽しいゲームです。

便利機能がアンロックされたり農場の経営が安定してきて大金持ちになっても、ゲーム内でタスクが完全にはなくならないタイプのゲームなので、配信向きでもあります。
ストリーマーさんの配信で見てプレイしてみようという人も多いかと思いますが、自宅や農場のハウジングとかレイアウトの完成形を考えるのが最も楽しい部分かもしれませんので、純粋にシミュレーションが好きでないと続かない部分もあります。

あとペニーがかわいい。
暖かい家族計画を夢見ながら町の子供たちに勉強を教える心優しき女性を農場に嫁として迎え入れて、彼女の実家のトレーラーハウスを立派な一軒家に建て直すのがこのゲームのメインコンテンツです。異論は認める。

review:
Это очень приятный, добрый пиксельный винил, местами, правда, мне показалось, душноватый. Контента много, даже очень много для подобного инди, и это прекрасно, но также много и бесполезной беготни туда - сюда, и это немного ломает кайф. Да есть коняга, и обелиски, но обелиски слишком дорогие, и в начале игры не доступны. Мое скромное имхо, обелиски должны быть дешевле в разы, а золотые часы  стоимостью 10кк - это за гранью разумного.
Если хочешь пройти на платину, то придется пофармить. Чтобы закрыть все достижения у меня ушло почти 200 часов, и их почти не жалко. Чтобы получить достижение "Совершенство" нужно совокупно выфармить порядка 15кк денех (обелиски + золотые часы + по мелочам), и это дохера, и это самая душная часть.
Лично у меня еще случился тупняк с достижением "Золотые руки", в обсуждениях в гайде не хватает солнечной панели и страусиного инкубатора, поэтому нужно крафтить все предметы с вики: https://stardewvalley.fandom.com/ru/wiki/%D0%98%D0%B7%D0%B3%D0%BE%D1%82%D0%BE%D0%B2%D0%BB%D0%B5%D0%BD%D0%B8%D0%B5_%D0%BF%D1%80%D0%B5%D0%B4%D0%BC%D0%B5%D1%82%D0%BE%D0%B2
И для тех, кто играет соло обручальное кольцо крафтить не нужно.

Лично у меня еще случился тупняк с достижением "Золотые руки", в обсуждениях в гайде не хватает солнечной панели и страусиного инкубатора, поэтому нужно крафтить все предметы с вики И для тех, кто играет соло обручальное кольцо крафтить не нужно.
review_english: This is a very pleasant, kind pixel vinyl, although in some places, I thought it was a bit stuffy. There is a lot of content, even a lot for an indie like this, and that’s great, but there’s also a lot of useless running back and forth, and that breaks the fun a little. Yes, there is a horse and obelisks, but obelisks are too expensive and are not available at the beginning of the game. My humble imho, obelisks should be several times cheaper, and a gold watch costing 10kk is beyond reasonable. If you want to get to platinum, you will have to farm. It took me almost 200 hours to complete all the achievements, and I hardly feel sorry for them. To get the “Perfection” achievement you need to collectively farm about 15k money (obelisks gold watches for little things), and that’s a lot, and that’s the most stuffy part. Personally, I still had a problem with the “Golden Hands” achievement, in the discussions in the guide there is not enough solar panel and ostrich incubator, so you need to craft all the items from the wiki. And for those who play solo, there is no need to craft a wedding ring.

review:
As someone who had bought this game years ago and didn't touch it much as I just couldn't bring myself to find much interest in a farming simulator, I have to say that was one of my biggest mistakes I have made in regards to judging a game by its cover.

This game is not just a farming simulator like it is commonly described, it has so much more within it through the storytelling of the characters, the cute aspect of raising animals and finding spirits in the community center to help restore it to its past glory, exploring mines and caves in not only the main town but also in other locations through the bus, and a lot more I have yet to discover apparently.

I genuinely cannot recommend this game enough to someone who just wants a calming game to play since it encourages all sorts of playstyles. From casual to challenging this game has been a blast. And to anyone who has played this game a lot and found themselves burnt out of the normal content I highly recommend looking into the modding community with this game as there are entirely fanmade cities and campaigns of this game that expand the game from its already long runtime into something completely timeless. I would give names of mods but I myself haven't been able to get into that aspect just yet as I have been wanting to continue my vanilla journey before venturing into that realm; but I highly recommend looking into it from the people I've talked to about this game.

review:
O jogo que te convence a largar o seu emprego corporativo para virar o trabalhador mais obcecado da história da agricultura.

A premissa é o maior clássico dos jogos aconchegantes: você está cansado da rotina esmagadora de trabalhar na corporação Joja, abre a carta de herança do seu avô e se muda para a Vila Pelicano para assumir uma fazenda caindo aos pedaços. O que começa com você plantando seis sementes de parsnita evolui para um império agropecuário que gera milhões de ouros por dia.

Prós:

O ciclo diário é uma droga altamente viciante. O clássico "só vou jogar mais um dia para ver o que nasceu" é o maior causador de noites em claro na comunidade gamer. Quando você percebe, já são 4 da manhã na vida real e você passou a noite inteira pescando bagres na chuva.

Conteúdo infinito e suporte lendário. O criador do jogo, Eric Barone (ConcernedApe), fez o jogo inteiramente sozinho (arte, música, programação) e continua lançando atualizações massivas e gratuitas há anos. Cada patch parece uma DLC paga de tanta coisa que adiciona.

Liberdade de estilo de jogo. Quer focar em ter 40 porcos e enriquecer vendendo óleo de trufa? Dá. Quer esquecer a fazenda e passar o dia descendo os 120 andares das minas matando monstros? Dá. Quer só focar em dar presentes para o Shane até ele parar de ser grosso com você e aceitar casar? É super válido.

A trilha sonora original é uma obra-prima de nostalgia e paz que cura qualquer ansiedade.

Contras:

A fobia do relógio de dormir. Se der 2 da manhã no jogo e você estiver a um centímetro de distância da sua cama, o seu personagem simplesmente desmaia no chão e o médico da cidade (ou um funcionário da Joja) te rouba 1.000 moedas de ouro para te carregar até o quarto. É o maior terror psicológico do jogo.

O primeiro ano pode ser um pouco estressante para quem sofre de ansiedade por eficiência. Querer completar o Centro Comunitário o mais rápido possível exige que você decore qual peixe aparece em qual estação, em qual horário e sob qual clima, transformando o jogo relaxante em uma planilha do Excel.

10/10. Comprei para relaxar depois do trabalho, hoje tenho uma fábrica automatizada de vinho de fruta antiga, um dinossauro de estimação e considero o prefeito Lewis o meu inimigo mortal por ele gastar o dinheiro público em uma estátua de ouro dele mesmo. Comprem sem medo.

A premissa é o maior clássico dos jogos aconchegantes você está cansado da rotina esmagadora de trabalhar na corporação Joja, abre a carta de herança do seu avô e se muda para a Vila Pelicano para assumir uma fazenda caindo aos pedaços. O que começa com você plantando seis sementes de parsnita evolui para um império agropecuário que gera milhões de ouros por dia. Prós O ciclo diário é uma droga altamente viciante. O clássico "só vou jogar mais um dia para ver o que nasceu" é o maior causador de noites em claro na comunidade gamer. Quando você percebe, já são 4 da manhã na vida real e você passou a noite inteira pescando bagres na chuva. Conteúdo infinito e suporte lendário. O criador do jogo, Eric Barone (ConcernedApe), fez o jogo inteiramente sozinho (arte, música, programação) e continua lançando atualizações massivas e gratuitas há anos. Cada patch parece uma DLC paga de tanta coisa que adiciona. Liberdade de estilo de jogo. Quer focar em ter 40 porcos e enriquecer vendendo óleo de trufa? Dá. Quer esquecer a fazenda e passar o dia descendo os 120 andares das minas matando monstros? Dá. Quer só focar em dar presentes para o Shane até ele parar de ser grosso com você e aceitar casar? É super válido. A trilha sonora original é uma obra-prima de nostalgia e paz que cura qualquer ansiedade. Contras A fobia do relógio de dormir. Se der 2 da manhã no jogo e você estiver a um centímetro de distância da sua cama, o seu personagem simplesmente desmaia no chão e o médico da cidade (ou um funcionário da Joja) te rouba 1.000 moedas de ouro para te carregar até o quarto. É o maior terror psicológico do jogo. O primeiro ano pode ser um pouco estressante para quem sofre de ansiedade por eficiência. Querer completar o Centro Comunitário o mais rápido possível exige que você decore qual peixe aparece em qual estação, em qual horário e sob qual clima, transformando o jogo relaxante em uma planilha do Excel. 10 10. Comprei para relaxar depois do trabalho, hoje tenho uma fábrica automatizada de vinho de fruta antiga, um dinossauro de estimação e considero o prefeito Lewis o meu inimigo mortal por ele gastar o dinheiro público em uma estátua de ouro dele mesmo. Comprem sem medo.
review_english: The game that convinces you to quit your corporate job and become the most obsessed worker in the history of agriculture. The premise is the greatest classic of cozy games. You are tired of the overwhelming routine of working at the Joja corporation, you open your grandfather's inheritance letter and move to Vila Pelicano to take over a crumbling farm. What starts with you planting six parsnite seeds evolves into an agricultural empire that generates millions of gold per day. Pros The daily cycle is a highly addictive drug. The classic "I'm just going to play one more day to see what happens" is the biggest cause of sleepless nights in the gaming community. Next thing you know, it's 4 am in real life and you've spent the entire night fishing for catfish in the rain. Infinite content and legendary support. The game's creator, Eric Barone (ConcernedApe), made the game entirely himself (art, music, programming) and has continued releasing massive, free updates for years. Each patch feels like paid DLC with so much it adds. Freedom of playing style. Do you want to focus on having 40 pigs and getting rich selling truffle oil? From the. Do you want to forget about the farm and spend the day descending the 120 floors of the mines killing monsters? From the. Do you want to just focus on giving Shane gifts until he stops being rude to you and agrees to get married? It's super valid. The original soundtrack is a masterpiece of nostalgia and peace that cures any anxiety. Cons Bedtime clock phobia. If it's 2am in the game and you're an inch away from your bed, your character simply passes out on the floor and the town doctor (or a Joja employee) steals 1,000 gold coins from you to carry you to your room. It's the biggest psychological horror in the game. The first year can be a little stressful for those who suffer from efficiency anxiety. Wanting to complete the Community Center as quickly as possible requires you to memorize which fish appear in which season, at what time and under what weather, turning the relaxing game into an Excel spreadsheet. 10 10. I bought it to relax after work, today I have an automated antique fruit wine factory, a pet dinosaur and I consider Mayor Lewis my mortal enemy because he spends public money on a golden statue of himself. Buy without fear.

review:
Das Farmspiel schlechthin, mit witzigen Setting aber auch erwachsenen Themen. Jeder NPC hat eine eigene Hintergrundgeschichte und einige Charaktere kann sogar daten und später heiraten was weitere Geschichten freischaltet. 

Die Geschichten unterscheiden sich hier aber auch von dem, was man in anderen Spielen sieht. So gibt es einen Soldat der wiederkehrt und mit seiner Familie hadert, einen Einsiedler der sich am Müll bedient oder einen Betrunkenen dem nach Hause hilft. Das sind halt Geschichten die sich andere so nicht trauen die dann aber alle gut enden und ein positives Gefühl zurücklassen. 

Man kann auch relativ einfach zusammenspielen und dieses Spiel ist berüchtigt dafür, dass alle Altersklassen von Oma bis zum Enkel dieses Spiel spielen und auch gut finden. Dabei gibt es in der Mine auch Kampf und Action und mit mehreren Spielern kann das Spiel so zusätzlich Spaß machen, da sich jeder spezialisieren kann. Damit andere Spieler mitspielen können, muss man ihnen auf seiner Farm ein kleines Haus bauen von wo aus die Spieler starten. Das ist ein witziges Detail, aber wirklich gut gemacht.

review_english: The farming game par excellence, with a funny setting but also adult themes. Each NPC has their own backstory and some characters can even date and later marry which unlocks further stories. The stories here also differ from what you see in other games. So there is a soldier who returns and quarrels with his family, a hermit who helps himself to the garbage or a drunk who helps him home. These are just stories that others don't dare to tell, but they all end well and leave you with a positive feeling. It's also relatively easy to play together and this game is notorious for the fact that all age groups from grandmas to grandchildren play and like this game. There is also combat and action in the mine and with several players the game can be even more fun because everyone can specialize. In order for other players to play, you have to build a small house for them on your farm from which the players start. It's a fun detail, but really well done.
